title: Although Ethics Classes Are Common Around the World
subtitle: 一门伦理课能改变行为吗？道德教育的真实边界

paragraphs.en: Although ethics classes are common around the world, scientists are unsure if their lessons can actually change behavior; evidence either way is weak, relying on contrived laboratory tests or sometimes unreliable self-reports. But a new study published in Cognition found that, in at least one real-world situation, a single ethics lesson may have had lasting effects. The researchers investigated one class session's impact on eating meat.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管道德课程在世界各地很常见，但科学家们不确定他们的课程是否真的能改变行为；无论哪种方式，证据都很薄弱，依赖于人为的实验室测试或有时不可靠的自我报告。但发表在《认知》杂志上的一项新研究发现，至少在一个现实世界的情况下，一堂道德课可能会产生持久的影响。研究人员调查了一节课对吃肉的影响。研究报告的合著者、加州大学河滨分校的哲学家埃里克·施维茨格贝尔（EricSc​​hwitzgebel）表示，他们选择这种特殊行为有三个原因：学生对这个话题的态度是多变且不稳定的，行为很容易衡量，道德文献在很大程度上同意少吃肉是好的，因为它可以减少环境危害和动物痛苦。

paragraphs.en: They chose this particular behavior for three reasons, according to study co-author Eric Schwitzgebel, a philosopher at the University of California, Riverside: students' attitudes on the topic are variable and unstable, behavior is easily measurable, and ethics literature largely agrees that eating less meat is good because it reduces environmental harm and animal suffering. Half of the students in four large philosophy classes read an article on the ethics of factoryfarmed meat, optionally watched an 11-minute video on the topic and joined a 50-minute discussion.
paragraphs.zh: 研究报告的合著者、加州大学河滨分校的哲学家埃里克·施维茨格贝尔（EricSc​​hwitzgebel）表示，他们选择这种特殊行为有三个原因：学生对这个话题的态度是多变且不稳定的，行为很容易衡量，道德文献在很大程度上同意少吃肉是好的，因为它可以减少环境危害和动物痛苦。四个大型哲学课上的一半学生阅读了一篇关于工厂化养殖肉类伦理的文章，选择性地观看了有关该主题的11分钟视频，并参加了50分钟的讨论。

paragraphs.en: The other half focused on charitable giving instead. Then, unknown to the students, the researchers studied their anonymized meal-card purchases for that semester — nearly 14,000 receipts for almost 500 students.
paragraphs.zh: 另一半则专注于慈善捐赠。然后，在学生们不知情的情况下，研究人员研究了他们在该学期购买的匿名餐卡——近500名学生的近14,000张收据。“这是一个很棒的数据集，”

paragraphs.en: “It's an awesome data set,” says Nina Strohminger, a psychologist who teaches business ethics at the University of Pennsylvania and was not involved in the study. Schwitzgebel predicted the intervention would have no effect; he had previously found that ethics professors do not differ from other professors on a range of behaviors, including voting rates, blood donation and returning library books.
paragraphs.zh: “这是一个很棒的数据集，”在宾夕法尼亚大学教授商业道德的心理学家尼娜·斯特罗明格（NinaStrohminger）说，她没有参与这项研究。施维茨格贝尔预测干预不会产生任何效果；他此前发现，伦理学教授在一系列行为上与其他教授没有什么不同，包括投票率、献血和归还图书馆书籍。

paragraphs.en: But among student subjects who discussed meat ethics, meal purchases containing meat decreased from 52 to 45 percent — and this effect held steady for the study's duration of several weeks. Purchases from the other group remained at 52 percent.
paragraphs.zh: 但在讨论肉类伦理的学生受试者中，购买含有肉类的膳食的比例从52%下降到45%，而且这种影响在研究的几周内保持稳定。来自另一组的购买量仍保持在52%。施维茨格贝尔说：“对于很小的干预来说，这实际上是一个相当大的影响。

paragraphs.en: “That's actually a pretty large effect for a pretty small intervention,” Schwitzgebel says. Psychologist Nina Strohminger at the University of Pennsylvania, who was not involved in the study, says she wants the effect to be real but cannot rule out some unknown confounding variable.
paragraphs.zh: 施维茨格贝尔说：“对于很小的干预来说，这实际上是一个相当大的影响。”宾夕法尼亚大学的心理学家尼娜·斯特罗明格（NinaStrohminger）没有参与这项研究，她说她希望这种效应是真实的，但不能排除一些未知的混杂变量。

paragraphs.en: And if real, she notes, it might be reversible by another nudge: “Easy come, easy go.” Schwitzgebel suspects the greatest impact came from social influence — classmates or teaching assistants leading the discussions may have shared their own vegetarianism, showing it as achievable or more common. Second, the video may have had an emotional impact.
paragraphs.zh: 她指出，如果是真的，这种情况可能会通过另一次推动而逆转：“来得容易，去得也快。”施维茨格贝尔怀疑最大的影响来自社会影响——主导讨论的同学或助教可能分享了他们自己的素食主义，表明它是可以实现的或更普遍的。其次，该视频可能产生了情感影响。

paragraphs.en: Least rousing, he thinks, was rational argument, although his co-authors say reason might play a bigger role. Now the researchers are probing the specific effects of teaching style, teaching assistants' eating habits and students' video exposure. Meanwhile Schwitzgebel — who had predicted no effect — will be eating his words.
paragraphs.zh: 他认为，最不令人兴奋的是理性论证，尽管他的合著者表示理性可能发挥更大的作用。现在研究人员正在探讨教学风格、助教的饮食习惯和学生视频曝光的具体影响。与此同时，施维茨格贝尔——他曾预测不会有任何效果——将食言。
